item.title: Ok. Sorry about that break. As a reward for your patience, here's the 273st eromem album to kick off your Mjonday.
item.description:
Just realized it's been 3 weeks since my last horni album. That's untenable. But imma try and defend it anyway. I had shit going on, lol. So here's the next installment, thank you for your patience. As usual, no OC here, just things I've found in the wild lands.
Queck
Ahhhh *toe beans*! Dr.Bees says hello from his perch!
It's not porn! That's saved elsewhere. Let's talk about porn, actually. I like it. Big surprise. Many many many people do as well. Now. I will again specify I am not a DOCTOR, PSYCHOLOGIST, OR PSYCHIATRIST, but in my own experience, porn can get in the way of a good time. It alters your view of a healthy sex life and makes gratification so finely tailored to your needs that you have a hard time enjoying the actual quality time you have to spend with others. Porn can be an addiction. I fully advocate enjoying porn, I think it's fun and a great way to find new things you may not be ready to dive into in your personal sex life. But like all gratification, be mindful of your consumption. You have to moderate your enjoyment and not lose touch with your life or your *partner's* wants/needs. Ok, that's it for today. Heres the archive! May your way be Green and Golden. https://imgur.com/a/vhx2cjG PS: And again. Big thank you to all the support I recieved as I got noisy at the staff and mods about suspensions and lack of transparency. I am still hoping to have a *conversation* with them about the experience so we can make this platform a little better.
item.dc:creator: Icouri

item.title: Just restored this 1934 Crosley grandfather clock radio
item.description:
Edit: I had no clue anyone would actually be interested in seeing this. I restore probably 150 radios and pieces like this every year. If there are those who wants to see more of this I'll continue to put more detailed posts about these processes This was donated to our museum recently. It is a early 30s Crosley grandfather clock radio. It's made out of a interesting material. Steamed pressed plywood to get the appearance of carved wood even though it's just crappy plywood veneer.
Is it just me or is this the dial sort of look Edward Gorey/edward scissorhands?
I polished the original electrolytic capacitor containers which are in the copper cans
Little spring pulled ribbon with pulleys adjusts the station indicator behind a lamp. The chassis was overhauled. All new capacitors, and lots of new wiring
This is a manual start synchronous motor. Meaning when you lose power it intentionally stops you'll know that your clock is incorrect.
The thin plywood had started to pull away from parts of the frame. So I reglued and clamped that to stabilize
item.dc:creator: oldradios19772000

item.title: Memes vol 503 Boobie
item.description:
Another edition of memes my boyfriend and I have sent each other- 1 year anniversary edition
Ms. Chompy
Me :(
“They say you want to be loved from your head to your toes until you start lovin toes”
I sent him a reel with a reel of this kid and said “I want a small fat child”
This just a fast track to the last of us
He sent me this while I was wearing this color
Looney Toons ass
Big Mama tax
Scruffy, Liza Jane and Froggy tax
Nessie tax. Happy Anniversary @chriscaprisun I love you!!!

item.title: 16 fav + my fav meme genre (and cat tax)
item.description:
Edit: Ok, I did an edit bc apparently my first 16th fav was created by a godawful racist. I can promise you that asshat has now been removed from my favourites folder. Instead, meet my new 16th favourite: bird whose every decision only worsened its situation. (If someone says the bird has done anything problematic, I'm gonna fucking scream)
One of my favourite meme genres is 'Big Cats Doing Small Cat Things'. Most cat owners have observed their fascination with playing and drinking water from the tap.
Doing the playful cute thing
Getting their head stuck...
... or other body parts.
Obligatory "ooooh, big stretch!"
No cat can resist a nice box.
Doing the "butt lick interruptus"
Boxes (and other fitable and sitable containers), a felidae family favourite.
Cat tax: Miss Charlotta: "There's still the white crap outside. I thought I told you to get rid of it." Me: Or you could stay inside. Miss Charlotta: "No, no no. Constant vigilance!" The first 11 years of her life, Charlie didn't like going out in winter. As soon as snow and freezing temperatures arrived, she stayed inside and didn't ask to be let out until sometime in March. But ever since 'The Boy Invasion' ( https://imgur.com/gallery/Di0vZTD ), she must check the outside. Every. Damn. Day.
item.dc:creator: Kittensandyarns

item.dc:creator: StevenNumber3

item.title: In 7+ years of indie game dev, we have never once sold out at a con, until now.
item.description:
I've been doing tabletop indie game dev since 2018 and have been to dozens of conventions and conferences. Never have I ever once sold out at an event...until now!
Sold over 7 boxes! When we first arrived I thought to myself, "oh man I definitely brought too many" I've been to so many cons where I've only sold like 5-10 games and left with a bittersweet taste in my mouth. Yes the people I met were amazing and I was able to have my games enjoyed by them, but it all felt like such a waste of time...
The person who I sold my final copy to. The game was so popular that I even ended up selling my demo copies at 50% off!
People even wanted my signature on the games
item.dc:creator: sysifuscorp

item.title: Government workers: don't forget!
item.description: Source: https://bsky.app/profile/taradublinrocks.bsky.social/post/3liu7m4ejqc2g --------------------------------------------------------------------- If there is an article that seems important but you cannot read, do not give up. Copy the title and put it in a search engine to find and alternative source that has pulled that story's text. archive.ph often allows you to put an address to the article to bypass this. To make things even simpler, there are block lists that you can add to UBlock to open these documents directly. You can use these links on PC, Mac and Android. You can drop this filter list directly into UBlock and it will allow you through (https://raw.githubusercontent.com/liamengland1/miscfilters/refs/heads/master/antipaywall.txt). This list is updated regularly, with an update just two days ago. I will provide a video that shows how to do this however, due to the compression methods here, it shows up a bit low quality but it is enough to easily figure it out. (https://imgur.com/gallery/land-of-free-to-yuh-72f1Fuq) It's recommended to use a browser other than Chrome in order to navigate. Recently, Google has been combatting against ad blockers (convenient, huh?) and from what I understand, they have removed extensions. Some on previous versions may still have access but it is only temporary and deemed unreliable on that basis. --------------------------------------------------------------------- NeatURL is an extension to help remove clutter and tracking from urls (https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/neat-url/). ClearURL is also an alternative (however I haven't tried it, it's #1 in the store) https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/clearurls/ If you're okay with manually enabling/disabling what is displayed, I recommend NoScript Security Suite to put full control into your hands. Note, it can be bothersome at first, but once you set permissions on each page that you visit frequently, you don't have to mess around with doing it over and over. Protection isn't meant to be convenient in this age, unfortunately. https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/noscript/ XCancelled will forward you to Twitter comments outside of the environment, allowing you to view information without being signed in. This is incredibly useful for news stories that contain "Tweets" so you can see their comments or download videos from their sources (with other tools) https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/nitter/ Load Reddit Images Directly does just that. Rather than clicking an image to slowly have it converted to a different format (many times unusable and needs to be converted back resulting in degradation), it will open in a new tab as .jpg https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/load-reddit-images-directly/ Chrome Mask will trick sites into thinking you're using Chrome for certain tasks. YouTube is a good example- to combat adblockers, they have limited their videos to those who they detect are using browsers that allow adblockers. There is a toggle switch for how you would like your browser to be announced. https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/chrome-mask Onetab is also useful but you can end up with tons and tons to "look over later" as I often do. https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/onetab/ Tab session manager- same as above. (I also have a hundreds here that I haven't gotten around to) https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/tab-session-manager/ For editing posts, if you do not have Photoshop, there is a browser-based alternative you can use completely for free with (practically) all of the bells and whistles. https://www.photopea.com/ If there is text on the screen and you cannot copy it, try Text-Grab. (my hotkey is alt+f) https://github.com/TheJoeFin/Text-Grab --------------------------------------------------------------------- Stay in the Fight! https://actionnetwork.org/forms/thank-you-for-taking-action-stay-in-the-fight/ Keep up with current threats: https://www.realtimefascism.com/ How far along are we to fascism? https://www.project2025.observer/ Support Ukraine! https://americancoalitionforukraine.org/ Another useful sub: https://old.reddit.com/r/Keep_Track/ Contact your representatives in Washington (yes, even if your representatives are Republicans. In fact, ESPECIALLY if they are) for any and all that concerns you. Here is a number for the Capitol switchboard, they'll patch you right through: (202)-224-3121 https://5calls.org/ On the Play store: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=org.a5calls.android.a5calls https://generalstrikeus.com/ Litigation Tracker: Legal Challenges to Trump Administration Actions https://www.justsecurity.org/107087/tracker-legal-challenges-trump-administration-actions/ Keep an eye on the Executive Actions & Legislation. Question each one. https://potustracker.us/ How much is he golfing? https://trumpgolftrack.com/ Keep up with everything happening here: https://old.reddit.com/r/politics/ https://old.reddit.com/r/fednews/ Pardons for criminals, by criminals (ongoing): https://www.justice.gov/pardon Pardons for criminals, by criminals (1st term): https://www.justice.gov/pardon/pardons-granted-president-donald-j-trump-2017-2021 Fox puppets laying out how to steal an election before actively commanding it: https://imgur.com/gallery/sQzDOlI Brought to you by: "The best people" https://www.dailykos.com/history/user/CajsaLilliehook
item.dc:creator: BlasphemousJones

item.title: 16th favorite, and next weekend's dinner
item.description: Ingredients 1 3-4 lb. boneless pork loin roast ⅔ C grated parmesan cheese ½ C honey 3 Tbsp soy sauce 1 Tbsp dried basil 1 Tbsp dried oregano 2 Tbsp chopped garlic 2 Tbsp olive oil ½ tsp salt 2 Tbs cornstarch ¼ C chicken broth 1 tsp sugar, optional Instructions Place roast in slow cooker. In a bowl combine cheese, honey, soy sauce, basil, oregano, garlic, oil, and salt. Pour mixture over the top of roast. Cover and cook on low for 5-6 hours or until thermometer reads 160 degrees. Remove meat and place on platter. Strain juices in slow cooker and put into a small sauce pan. Mix cornstarch and chicken broth together. Add into strained juices and stir until thickened over medium heat. Add in a teaspoon of sugar if sauce needs to be a bit sweeter, optional Slice roast and serve with glaze. [source](http://www.yourhomebasedmom.com/slow-cooker-parmesan-honey-pork-roast/)
item.dc:creator: ScoundrelPDX
